ModuleName: Applied Data Structures: Blockchain, Digital Currency, and Beyond

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: Outlines the module's subject matter across three areas—foundational data structures (hash tables, trees, graphs), blockchain architecture (linked lists, cryptographic hashing, Merkle trees), and digital currency systems (wallets, UTXO model)—giving learners and instructors a structured overview of the topics to be studied.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A topic outline covering how core data structures—hash tables, trees, and graphs—underpin real-world systems like blockchain and digital currency, giving learners and instructors a conceptual roadmap of the module's progression from foundational concepts to applied use cases.
